operating_state: Net sales increased $1.9 billion to $21.6 billion driven by organic growth across all segments, with commercial aerospace aftermarket leading recovery through increased flight hours and air traffic normalization. Services revenue expanded to 27.9% of total sales from 26.2% prior year, reflecting stronger aftermarket activity despite May IAM work stoppage impacts.
cost_structure: Cost of sales increased but improved as percentage of sales to 79.7% from 81.8% prior year, benefiting from absence of prior year contract termination charges. Operating margin expanded to 9.9% from 2.7% prior year with defense achieving double-digit margins on favorable international program production mix. Commercial aerospace profitability improved on higher aftermarket volume despite unfavorable mix and increased tariff costs.
material_events: IAM work stoppage at Pratt & Whitney from May 5-27, 2025 reduced engine deliveries before full production resumed in June. Company recorded customer bankruptcy charge during second quarter and repaid debt while maintaining quarterly dividend at $0.68 per share. Credit rating outlooks improved to stable from negative at both major agencies during the period.
macro_exposures: U.S. government imposed tariffs on imports from all countries since February 2025, with counter-tariffs materially impacting commercial OEM profitability particularly in large commercial engine operations. Company maintains significant foreign cash holdings creating ongoing currency and repatriation considerations. Defense bookings reflect continued geopolitical demand for missile defense and radar systems.
forward_outlook: Executive Order on Iron Dome for America allocated funding for next-generation missile defense system development with DoD receiving supplementary funding through 2029. RTX projects full-year 2025 cash impact of $1.1-1.3 billion related to Powder Metal Matter including customer compensation and credits. Company maintains share repurchase authorization while pursuing digital transformation initiatives.
uncertainty_profile: Powder Metal Matter continues requiring accelerated fleet inspections with ongoing supply chain disruptions from geopolitical conflicts and labor shortages, representing primary near-term cash flow uncertainty. Compliance monitoring requirements under DOJ and SEC agreements extend three years from monitor engagement. Customer bankruptcy exposure emerged as new risk factor with geopolitical tensions creating operational uncertainties.
